content.full_text:
State of California SUPERSEDED
Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
107 SOUTH BROADWAY • • • SUITE 2140A • • • LOS ANGELES, 90012

April 25, 1977

Ines Del Campo
City Clerk
P. O. Box 550
Arroyo Grande, CA 93420

Dear Ms. Del Campo:

This letter will confirm my advice to you by telephone
today concerning the campaign disclosure provisions
of the Political Reform Act.

You asked whether the local Chamber of Commerce will
incur any reporting obligations under the Act by virtue
of sponsoring a public meeting at which all candidates
in your May 10th election will appear. I advised that
as long as the Chamber provides a forum for all of the
candidates on an equal basis and does not make any
expenditures to advocate the election of any of the
candidates, that it will not be required to report
its payments in connection with the meeting. Further-
more, the candidates need not report an allocated
portion of the expenditures made by the Chamber as an
in-kind contribution.

If you have further questions, please contact me.

Sincerely yours,


Nan Emmerson
Special Compliance Representative


NE:jg


SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660 • LOS ANGELES (213) 620-5196

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a local Chamber of Commerce sponsoring a public meeting for all candidates must report related expenditures and whether candidates must report an allocated portion as an in-kind contribution.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: If the Chamber of Commerce provides an equal forum for all candidates without making expenditures to advocate for any candidate's election, the Chamber need not report its meeting-related payments, and candidates need not report an allocated portion as an in-kind contribution.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present; content is in letter format. Advice was originally given by telephone and this letter confirms it. Recipient is City Clerk seeking guidance on local campaign event compliance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a local Chamber of Commerce sponsoring a public meeting for all candidates must report related expenditures and whether candidates must report an allocated portion as an in-kind contribution.

CONCLUSION: If the Chamber of Commerce provides an equal forum for all candidates without making expenditures to advocate for any candidate's election, the Chamber need not report its meeting-related payments, and candidates need not report an allocated portion as an in-kind contribution.

Box 550 Arroyo Grande, CA 93420 Dear Ms. Del Campo: This letter will confirm my advice to you by telephone today concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act. You asked whether the local Chamber of Commerce will incur any reporting obligations under the Act by virtue of sponsoring a public meeting at which all candidates in your May 10th election will appear. I advised that as long as the Chamber provides a forum for all of the candidates on an equal basis and does not make any expenditures to advocate the election of any of the candidates, that it will not be required to report its payments in connection with the meeting. Further- more, the candidates need not report an allocated portion of the expenditures made by the Chamber as an in-kind contribution. If you have further questions, please contact me. Sincerely yours, Nan Emmerson Special Compliance Representative NE:jg SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660 • LOS ANGELES (213) 620-5196
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming that a Chamber of Commerce-sponsored public candidate forum with equal access for all candidates is not subject to campaign disclosure reporting requirements if the Chamber makes no advocacy expenditures.
